overview: These snails appear to be Variable Forest Snails (Indrella ampulla), also known as Red Giant Snails, which are endemic to the Western Ghats in India. The purple structures shown are their phalluses, which evert during a specialized 'face-to-face' mating process. This species is polymorphic, meaning their soft body parts can be various colors, including red, orange, or yellow. Their breeding season typically coincides with the monsoon season, lasting from May through September. Instagram +2 3 sites The Variable Forest Snail, also known as the Red Giant Snail ...
